content.text:
[Fri 2026-04-17 12:29 EDT] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]: You are working on Recipe Rip - a recipe extraction iOS/web app. 

The project is at: ~/RecipeRipIOS/RecipeRip/

Currently the app:
- Has a basic UI with header "🍴 Recipe Rip", tabs (Rip/Saved), and input field
- Works on iOS Simulator but emoji doesn't render on simulator (works on real iPhone)
- Has a loading animation that needs to be reviewed
- The web version at http://100.103.22.35:8789 is the reference

Your tasks:
1. Review the current App.js and identify what improvements to make
2. Focus on: better UI polish, smooth animations, proper emoji rendering
3. Make concrete code changes and test them
4. Report back what you did

Important notes:
- The loading screen version at commit a4f7bda is GOLDEN - do not change it
- The iOS app uses React Native + Expo
- Metro bundler runs on port 8081
- Build with: cd ~/RecipeRipIOS/RecipeRip/ios && xcodebuild -workspace RecipeRip.xcworkspace -scheme RecipeRip -configuration Debug -destination "platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 17 Pro" build
- Install with: xcrun simctl install booted ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData/RecipeRip-*/Build/Products/Debug-iphonesimulator/RecipeRip.app && xcrun simctl launch booted com.bobbygalletta.reciperip

Start by reading App.js and making useful improvements!

plan.step: Inspect App.js and related UI code
Implement UI polish and emoji rendering fixes without touching golden loading screen
Build and test on iOS Simulator, then summarize results

content.thinking:
**Inspecting app improvements**

I need to check the repo, particularly the App.js file. It's important to keep the loading screen intact, so I won’t change that section. I should look for improvements too, like removing duplicate styles and ensuring proper emoji rendering—maybe I can use an image asset instead? I also want to inspect the assets to see if the logo image exists. Time to search and find what I need!

content.thinking:
**Considering icon use**

I'm thinking about whether to use an icon image instead of a fork emoji. It could be useful to inspect a web reference using a browser, though maybe it's not strictly necessary. I’m also considering checking the current app screenshot via the browser, given it's a web version. Overall, it feels like exploring these options could provide some helpful insights!
